question: A vertical mirror is placed on the right side of the word 'CAT'. What will be the order of letters seen in the mirror?
explanation: A vertical mirror reverses the left-right order. Therefore, CAT becomes TAC.
shortcut: Vertical mirror = Reverse left to right.
learning_tip: In mirror image questions, first reverse the sequence before considering the shape of characters.

question: A vertical mirror is placed to the right of the number 4825. Which sequence appears in the mirror?
explanation: The order reverses from left to right. Therefore, 4825 becomes 5284.
shortcut: Read the number from right to left.
learning_tip: Unless stated otherwise, only reverse the sequence—not the numeric value.

question: A mirror image formed by a vertical mirror shows reversal in which direction?
explanation: A vertical mirror reverses the left and right sides of an object.
shortcut: Vertical mirror → Left ↔ Right.
learning_tip: This is the fundamental concept behind all mirror image questions.

question: Which English capital letter looks the same even after a vertical mirror image?
explanation: The capital letter A is vertically symmetrical and appears unchanged in a vertical mirror.
shortcut: Think of vertically symmetrical letters.
learning_tip: Letters like A, H, I, M, O, T, U, V, W, X and Y are vertically symmetrical.

question: Which digit remains unchanged in a vertical mirror (standard digital representation excluded)?
explanation: The digit 0 is vertically symmetrical and appears unchanged.
shortcut: Remember symmetrical digits.
learning_tip: Only a few digits remain unchanged depending on the font style used.

question: If a vertical mirror is placed on the left side of the word 'BOOK', what is the order of letters in the mirror image?
explanation: Whether the mirror is on the left or right, the reflected sequence is reversed. BOOK becomes KOOB.
shortcut: Mirror always reverses left-right orientation.
learning_tip: The mirror's position changes the viewing direction, but the sequence is still reversed.

question: A mirror is placed to the right of the sequence @#$%. Which is the mirror image?
explanation: The sequence reverses from left to right. Therefore, @#$% becomes %$#@.
shortcut: Reverse the entire sequence.
learning_tip: Treat symbols exactly like letters when finding mirror images.

question: Which of the following is always true for a vertical mirror image?
options: The size of the object changes
explanation: A vertical mirror reverses only the left and right directions. The size and orientation remain unchanged.
shortcut: Vertical mirror = Left ↔ Right only.
learning_tip: This is the most fundamental mirror image concept asked in placement exams.

question: Which of the following capital letters has both vertical and horizontal symmetry?
explanation: The capital letter H has both vertical and horizontal lines of symmetry.
shortcut: Think of letters that look identical when folded vertically and horizontally.
learning_tip: Letters like H, I, O and X have both vertical and horizontal symmetry.

question: A vertical mirror is placed to the right of the number 76021. Which sequence appears in the mirror?
explanation: Mirror images reverse the sequence from left to right. Hence, 76021 becomes 12067.
shortcut: Read the sequence from right to left.
learning_tip: Always reverse the order before considering individual digit shapes.

question: A vertical mirror is placed beside the word 'LEVEL'. What is the mirror sequence?
explanation: The mirror sequence is LEVEL reversed, which is still LEVEL because it is a palindrome.
shortcut: Palindromes remain the same when their order is reversed.
learning_tip: A palindrome reads the same forward and backward, although individual letters may not be mirror-symmetrical.

question: A person is facing North and looking into a vertical mirror placed in front of them. In the mirror image, the person appears to face which direction?
explanation: A mirror reverses the front-back orientation. Therefore, a person facing North appears to face South in the mirror.
shortcut: Facing direction appears opposite in the mirror.
learning_tip: Direction-based mirror questions are common in placement reasoning tests.

question: A clock shows 4:20. What time will its mirror image approximately show?
explanation: Mirror time = 11:60 − Actual Time. Therefore, 11:60 − 4:20 = 7:40.
shortcut: Mirror Time = 11:60 − Given Time.
learning_tip: This shortcut works for analog clock mirror image questions.

question: A mirror is placed to the right of the sequence A#7@P. Which sequence represents the mirror image?
explanation: The sequence is simply reversed from right to left. Therefore, A#7@P becomes P@7#A.
shortcut: Reverse the complete sequence.
learning_tip: Treat letters, numbers and symbols exactly the same while reversing.

question: Which capital English letter has neither vertical symmetry nor horizontal symmetry?
explanation: The capital letter F has neither vertical nor horizontal symmetry.
shortcut: Remember common asymmetrical letters like F, G, J, L, P, R.
learning_tip: Knowing symmetrical and asymmetrical letters saves time in mirror image questions.

question: A vertical mirror is placed to the right of the sequence 'A2B5C8'. Which is the correct mirror image sequence?
explanation: The entire sequence reverses from right to left. Therefore, A2B5C8 becomes 8C5B2A.
shortcut: Reverse the order of all characters.
learning_tip: Mixed letter-number mirror questions are frequently asked in TCS, Infosys, Accenture and Cognizant placement exams.

question: Which of the following capital letters has only horizontal symmetry?
explanation: The capital letter C has horizontal symmetry but not vertical symmetry in standard block lettering.
shortcut: C looks the same when folded horizontally.
learning_tip: Differentiate between vertical symmetry and horizontal symmetry while solving mirror image questions.

question: A vertical mirror is placed to the right of the sequence 'M7@K2#'. Which is the correct mirror image?
explanation: A vertical mirror reverses the order of the entire sequence. Hence M7@K2# becomes #2K@7M.
shortcut: Reverse every character from right to left.
learning_tip: Letters, numbers and symbols are all reversed in order.

question: A clock shows 9:35. What time will its mirror image show?
shortcut: Mirror Time = 11:60 − Actual Time.
learning_tip: This shortcut is valid only for analog clock mirror questions.

question: A vertical mirror is placed beside the word 'PYTHON'. Which sequence appears in the mirror?
explanation: Reverse the order of the letters. PYTHON becomes NOHTYP.
shortcut: Read the word from right to left.
learning_tip: Ignore pronunciation; simply reverse the sequence.

question: A person is facing East while looking into a vertical mirror. In the mirror image, the person appears to face which direction?
explanation: A vertical mirror reverses the facing direction. East appears as West.
shortcut: Mirror shows the opposite facing direction.
learning_tip: North ↔ South and East ↔ West in mirror-facing questions.

question: Which of the following is NOT affected by a vertical mirror image?
explanation: A mirror changes orientation but does not change the actual shape or size of the object.
shortcut: Mirror changes appearance, not dimensions.
learning_tip: A mirror reflects—it does not distort.

question: Which capital English letter has vertical symmetry but does NOT have horizontal symmetry?
explanation: The letter A has vertical symmetry only. H, O and X have both vertical and horizontal symmetry.
shortcut: A is vertically symmetrical only.
learning_tip: Memorizing symmetrical letters speeds up mirror-image questions.

question: A vertical mirror is placed to the right of the sequence '9A#6P2'. Which of the following represents the correct mirror image?
explanation: Reverse the sequence completely. Therefore, 9A#6P2 becomes 2P6#A9.
shortcut: Always reverse the complete sequence from right to left.
learning_tip: Mixed sequences of letters, numbers and symbols are among the most common mirror-image questions in placement exams.

question: A vertical mirror is placed to the right of the sequence 'AB12@XY9'. Which of the following is the correct mirror image?
explanation: A vertical mirror reverses the complete sequence from right to left. Therefore, AB12@XY9 becomes 9YX@21BA.
shortcut: Mirror image = Reverse the entire sequence from right to left.
learning_tip: For mixed sequences containing letters, numbers and symbols, simply reverse the order first. Only if the question provides figures should you additionally consider the mirrored shape of individual characters.
